rationale_overall: Quiet rebalance at cap=5: every one of the five held theses got reinforced inside the Q1 2026 lookback, and no candidate from robotics or demographics surfaced a Q1-dated commercial milestone strong enough to displace a held name at this tight cap. NVDA's Q4 FY26 print on Feb 25, 2026 was a record blowout - $68.1B revenue +73% YoY, Data Center $62.3B +75% YoY, Networking $11.0B +263% YoY on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp - confirming the AI surge is still compounding through fiscal year-end. RKLB's Q4 2025 print on Feb 26, 2026 delivered record $180M Q4 revenue (full-year $602M +38%, backlog $1.85B +73% YoY) but the Neutron timeline slipped again after a January 2026 tank rupture in qualification testing (manufacturing defect in a hand-laid autoclave joint, fix is automated fiber placement) - the slip is the one mixed signal but the small-launch and space-systems franchise is intact and the thesis is not breached. IONQ's Q4 2025 print on Feb 25, 2026 hit $61.9M revenue +429% YoY, sold the gen-5 100-qubit system to KISTI, and expanded the QuantumBasel agreement past $60M over four years and four generations - the quantum buildup-phase signal is strengthening. URA's hyperscaler-nuclear thesis is reinforced by Meta's Jan 9, 2026 announcement to procure up to 6.6 GW of nuclear in the PJM market and by NexGen Energy's disclosure of uranium-supply talks with data center providers for the Rook 1 project - the AI-nuclear cadence is now an established pattern. LNG continues to ride the Russia-Ukraine geopolitical-energy thesis with European demand on long-dated take-or-pay contracts. Wave coverage unchanged: AI (NVDA), nuclear (URA), rockets/spacecraft (RKLB), geopolitical-energy (LNG), quantum (IONQ) - five distinct buckets, one ticker each, all in buildup or surge per the profile. At cap=5 the bar to swap is high and no candidate cleared it this final rebalance of the window.
